name: Adnan Tawkul
title: Nerual Engineer | Biomedical Engineer
summary: M.Sc. Neural Engineering graduate with 4 years of industry experience in life-science automation, computer vision, Python-based image processing, and technical validation of complex automated systems. Experienced in working with real-world imaging workflows, camera-based systems, noisy sensor data, and automated laboratory platforms under practical deployment conditions. Strong background in machine learning, OpenCV, TensorFlow, signal processing, system testing, and troubleshooting. Combines hands-on industrial automation experience with computer vision and AI skills to build reliable perception and validation pipelines for real-world technical systems.
studies:
Saarbruecken 04/2022 – 05/2025 HTW Saar M.Sc. Neural Engineering Focus: Machine learning, EEG signal processing, image processing, neural data analysis, neurotechnological systems, Python-based biomedical data analysis. Relevant work: EEG preprocessing, feature extraction, ML model evaluation, biomedical signal interpretation. 
Saarbruecken 10/2018 – 04/2022 HTW Saar B.Sc. Biomedical Engineering Focus: medical technology systems, measurement technology, biological processes, technical validation, and data-driven analysis.
professions:
07/2022 – Present
Sartorius

Service Engineer
•Contributed to the technical development, testing, and validation of AI-related functionality for the Sartorius CellCelector platform, combining Python, image processing, system testing, and real-world user feedback.
Jena, Germany
•Developed a Python-based computer vision algorithm for circle detection, applying image processing techniques to automate feature recognition in microscopy-based workflows.
•Converted ambiguous field failures into structured technical investigations, reproducible test scenarios, and actionable improvements for R&D, software, and hardware teams.
•Worked at the intersection of users, engineering, and product development, translating customer pain points into concrete system improvements.
•Delivered high stakes troubleshooting, calibration, and validation of automated laboratory systems in international customer environments, developing strong ownership, execution discipline, and first-principles problem-solving.
•Supported over 200 systems across Europe.
projects:
Face-Aware FlowMag for Micro-Expression Spotting⁠

https://github.com/AdnanTawkul/face-aware-flowmag-microexpression
•Developed a computer vision pipeline for micro-expression spotting using optical flow, motion magnification, and face-aware regularization.
•Applied transfer learning and image/​video preprocessing techniques to analyze subtle facial motion patterns.
•Built the project as a neural engineering application connecting computer vision, biomedical signal interpretation, and ML-based analysis.
Pneumonia Detection using Deep Learning⁠

https://github.com/AdnanTawkul/X-Ray_DeepLerningProject
•Built a deep learning model for pneumonia classification from chest X-ray images.
•Implemented preprocessing, data augmentation, class imbalance handling, model training, evaluation, and inference.
•Evaluated model performance using relevant classification metrics and improved robustness through dataset preparation and validation.
Wearable Vitals Validation Pipeline⁠

https://github.com/AdnanTawkul/wearable-vitals-validation
•Developed a modular validation pipeline for wearable sensor data.
•Implemented signal preprocessing, feature extraction, quality checks, and structured evaluation of physiological measurements.
•Focused on reliability, noisy real-world data, and reproducible biomedical signal analysis.

Skills
Programming & Software Engineering: Python, Git, GitHub, GitHub Desktop, PyTorch, CustomTkinter, PyInstaller, YAML configuration, modular pipeline development, reproducible project workflows.
Artificial Intelligence & Computer Vision: Deep learning, CNNs, ResNet50, MobileNetV3, VGG16, YOLO, optical flow, motion magnification, transfer learning, model adaptation, medical image classification, object detection, facial motion analysis.
Biomedical & Neural Engineering: Biomedical signal processing, wearable sensor validation, PPG analysis, heart-rate extraction, sensor synchronization, time-series analysis, medical imaging, neurotechnology applications.
Machine Learning Evaluation: Accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC-ROC, confusion matrix, RMSE, Pearson correlation, Bland-Altman analysis, bias estimation, error analysis.
Data & Image Processing: NumPy/Pandas-style workflows, MATLAB, JSON data handling, preprocessing, timestamp alignment, resampling, peak detection, data augmentation, class imbalance handling, Canny edge detection, thresholding, Gaussian blur, circularity detection.
Product & AI Tools: Local LLM integration, Ollama, resume automation, DOCX/PDF export, desktop app development, UI/UX iteration, versioned releases, post-release debugging.
structured_evidence:
Evidence 1: Face-Aware FlowMag for Micro-Expression Spotting
Type: Project
Context / situation: Research-engineering project adapting self-supervised motion magnification to subtle facial micro-expression spotting on CASME II.
Tools / technologies: Python, PyTorch, optical flow, CASME II, facial landmark masks, LBP-TOP, SVM
Methods / actions: Fine-tuned a pretrained FlowMag-style motion magnification model, added face-aware regularization with landmark-based masks, compared baseline inference, test-time adaptation, and face-aware training variants.
Outcome / purpose: Produced a more spatially meaningful facial motion amplification pipeline and supported downstream micro-expression evaluation through motion analysis and feature-based classification.
Metrics / proof: Research repository, loss formulation, baseline comparison, motion error analysis, downstream LBP-TOP + SVM evaluation workflow.
Relevant job signals: computer vision, deep learning, optical flow, model adaptation, transfer learning, algorithms, validation, neural engineering

---

Evidence 2: Chest X-Ray Pneumonia Classification using Deep Learning
Type: Project
Context / situation: Small university project classifying chest X-rays as Normal or Pneumonia.
Tools / technologies: Python, CNN models, ResNet50, MobileNetV3, VGG16, CUDA, YAML config.
Methods / actions: Built a training pipeline with data augmentation, class imbalance handling, early stopping, model evaluation, and inference.
Outcome / purpose: Trained and tested deep learning models for binary medical image classification.
Metrics / proof: Generated accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score, AUC-ROC, confusion matrix, and accuracy plots.
Relevant job signals: Computer vision, deep learning, model evaluation, medical image classification, reproducible ML workflow.

---

Evidence 3: Wearable Vitals Validation Pipeline
Type: Project
Context / situation: Bachelor thesis project validating Garmin Fenix 6 heart-rate data against a professional Shimmer PPG reference system.
Tools / technologies: Python, MATLAB, JSON, PPG signals, Garmin Fenix 6, Shimmer sensor, NumPy/Pandas-style signal workflow, statistical validation.
Methods / actions: Built a modular pipeline for data ingestion, preprocessing, timestamp alignment, peak detection, HR extraction, resampling, and cross-correlation synchronization.
Outcome / purpose: Created a reproducible biomedical signal validation workflow to compare wearable sensor accuracy against reference measurements.
Metrics / proof: Pearson r: 0.388, RMSE: 6.32 bpm, bias: -3.18 bpm, estimated time offset: -29 s, 95% limits of agreement: [-13.89, 7.53] bpm.
Relevant job signals: Biomedical signal processing, wearable validation, time-series analysis, sensor synchronization, statistical evaluation, reproducible Python engineering.

---

Evidence 4: ResuBuilder, AI Resume and Cover Letter Builder Desktop App
Type: Project
Context / situation: Job seekers often waste time manually adapting CVs and cover letters for each job application. I built ResuBuilder to automate this process locally, without relying on cloud-based AI tools or exposing personal career data.
Tools / technologies: Python, CustomTkinter, Ollama, local LLMs, DOCX export, PDF export, PyInstaller, Git, GitHub, GitHub Desktop.
Methods / actions: Designed and built a desktop application that analyzes job descriptions, rewrites resume content, generates tailored cover letters, supports multiple UI themes, exports application documents, and packages the app as a Windows executable. Managed development through Git branches, versioned releases, and post-release fixes.
Outcome / purpose: Created a working AI-powered desktop tool that helps users generate more targeted job application documents faster, while keeping resume data local on the user’s machine.
Metrics / proof: 
Relevant job signals: LLM, AI, Python, Product development, UI/UX, Git

---

Evidence 5: Computer Vision Method for Glass Capillary Detection and Position Coordinate Estimation
Type: Project
Context / situation: Manual glass capillary calibration on the CellCelector requires X-Y positioning and Z-position adjustment after capillary replacement. This project aimed to automate capillary detection from microscope camera images and support future automated calibration.
Tools / technologies: YOLO, PyTorch, Roboflow, Computer Vision, Edge Detection, Binary Thresholding, Gaussian Blur, Circularity Detection, Microsoft Visual Studio, CUDA
Methods / actions: Trained YOLOv5 models to detect glass capillaries under different lighting, magnification, focus, and capillary-size conditions. Labeled microscope image datasets, trained separate models for capillary detection and focus classification, then combined YOLO bounding boxes with classical image-processing methods to calculate the capillary center point.
Outcome / purpose: Built a proof-of-concept algorithm that detects glass capillaries and estimates their X-Y position. The second model also supported Z-position estimation by identifying the frame where the capillary was in focus.
Metrics / proof: Trained YOLOv5 models on up to 331 training and 83 validation images, tested on separate unseen images, and achieved 93.2% to 96.6% average precision at IoU 0.5. The final method detected capillaries in 59 out of 60 test images and was also tested on video input for X-Y movement and Z-focus detection.
Relevant job signals: Computer vision and object detection, Deep learning model training, Image preprocessing and feature extraction, Dataset labeling and validation, Model evaluation with real metrics, Python / PyTorch / YOLO workflow, Practical problem solving under noisy imaging conditions, Scientific documentation, Ability to connect AI models to physical hardware workflows
